_note: Run #6 — install hardening + SMCP envelope. Fragment-scoped tasks: each replaces a SINGLE function in mcp_install_envelope.py rather than rewriting the whole module. Run #5 confirmed LLM coder models truncate beyond ~300 lines. Tasks listed in priority order — the first 3 target the v0 misses (path_traversal_in_fs, capability_assertion_outside_contract, old_nonce_after_eviction).

tasks.title: Path normalisation in InstallValidator.check_runtime (fs branch only)
tasks.description: v0 InstallValidator.check_runtime() naively does startswith() on fs paths, so 'fs:/repo/../etc/passwd' passes the 'starts with /repo/' check (att-inst-008). Replace ONLY the fs branch of check_runtime so it: (a) calls os.path.normpath on the requested path before comparing, (b) re-checks the normalised path is still under one of the granted prefixes, (c) refuses paths that contain '..' literal segments before normalisation as well (defensive). Keep the capability and egress branches UNCHANGED. Return the FULL module — copy every other class/function unchanged.
tasks.hypothesis: Path normalisation closes the traversal gap without false positives on legit paths (ben-inst-005 fs:/repo/README.md still passes).

tasks.title: Cross-contract capability check in EnvelopeVerifier.verify
tasks.description: v0 EnvelopeVerifier.verify() does NOT check that the envelope's capability_assertion is one the sender's InstallContract granted (att-env-007). Extend EnvelopeVerifier to take an optional `contracts: dict[sender_uuid, InstallContract]` map at __init__ AND to consult it during verify. Add a new ATTRIBUTE on the verifier (do not break the existing constructor signature with positional changes — keyword-arg with default empty dict is fine). Inside verify(), after HMAC + hash + replay checks, also check `if contracts and sender_uuid in contracts: assert capability_assertion in contracts[sender_uuid].granted_capabilities` — refuse with reason on mismatch. Keep ALL other methods + classes unchanged.
tasks.hypothesis: Cross-module wiring catches envelopes that claim a capability outside the sender's user-granted contract — a privilege escalation attempt.

tasks.title: Persistent nonce log to defeat cache-eviction replays (replace check_replay)
tasks.description: v0 EnvelopeVerifier.check_replay uses an OrderedDict bounded by nonce_cache_size — once full, oldest nonces are evicted and become re-acceptable. att-env-008 exploits this by filling the cache then replaying an old nonce. Replace check_replay with a hybrid: keep the in-memory recent-window cache (still bounded), AND add a Bloom-filter-like primitive (stdlib: a set of nonce hashes truncated to 8 bytes — false-positive rate ~0 at our scales) that retains nonces over a longer time horizon (e.g. 24h). On verify, check both. Periodically rotate the long-horizon set (don't grow forever). Keep wrap() and verify() unchanged — only check_replay is replaced.
tasks.hypothesis: Long-horizon nonce log catches replay attacks beyond the eviction window without unbounded memory growth.

tasks.title: Manifest capability inflation warning (validate_manifest only)
tasks.description: att-inst-011 has a manifest signed correctly by a trusted key but declaring extremely broad capabilities (declared_capabilities=['exec_shell', 'sampling', ...], required_egress=['*'], required_fs_access=['/']). Currently validate_manifest returns ok=True. Add: when ok=True, also surface a list of WARNINGS (not failures) for over-broad declarations: capabilities containing 'exec' or 'shell' or 'sudo'; required_egress containing '*'; required_fs_access containing '/' or '~'. Return tuple becomes (ok, problems, warnings) — but to preserve API compatibility, encode warnings INSIDE the problems list with a 'warning:' prefix, KEEP the ok=True flag. The harness's att-inst-011 expects expected_block=false (because the signature IS valid), but a future task could escalate this to full block. For this task, just emit the warnings.
tasks.hypothesis: Warnings surface adversarial-pattern manifests for human review without false-blocking signed packages.

tasks.title: Homoglyph defence in egress branch of check_runtime
tasks.description: att-inst-010 uses 'exarnple.com' (rn looking like m) to bypass the egress 'example.com' grant. Add a normalisation step in the egress branch of check_runtime that uses a small homoglyph mapping (only the most common pairs: rn→m, vv→w, l→I in some fonts but skip — keep the table small) and rejects when the normalised form coincidentally matches a granted egress. Be defensive — the goal is to flag suspicious lookalikes, not perfect detection. Only modify the egress branch.
tasks.hypothesis: Even a 5-pair homoglyph table catches the most common typosquatting attacks on egress whitelists.

tasks.title: Schema version + migration helpers in InstallContract
tasks.description: Add a SCHEMA_VERSION constant at module level + serialisation helpers on InstallContract (to_json, from_json) that include the schema version. Add a _MIGRATIONS dict mapping (from_version, to_version) → migration function for future schema changes. From_json reads the version and applies migrations sequentially up to SCHEMA_VERSION. Provide a noop migration v1→v1 as the default. The harness scenarios don't directly test this, but it's a robustness improvement that future deployments will need. Don't break the dataclass signature; add the (de)serialisation as classmethods/methods.
tasks.hypothesis: Schema migration support is non-negotiable for a state-bearing security control deployed across many sessions.
